story.heading: Supreme Court Rules Against Presidential Tariff Authority as Trump Floats New Nominations
story.summary: The Supreme Court has struck down President Donald Trump's global tariff policy, ruling that the executive branch lacks the authority to impose such trade penalties under current emergency laws. Following the decision, the President criticized the judiciary while suggesting Senator Ted Cruz as a potential future Supreme Court nominee.

segments.heading: Supreme Court Ruling on Tariffs
segments.text: In a significant legal setback for the administration, the Supreme Court ruled that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act does not grant the President the power to impose global tariffs. Chief Justice John Roberts, writing for the majority, emphasized that the power to tax and impose tariffs is constitutionally reserved for Congress. The ruling nullified a signature economic policy that had generated billions in revenue but was also linked to market volatility and increased costs for U.S. consumers.

citations.quote: Fulfilling that role, we hold that (the International Emergency Economic Powers Act) does not authorize the President to impose tariffs.
The justices, including two of the president’s appointees, struck down Trump’s signature economic policy: his global tariffs.

segments.heading: Executive Response and Economic Context
segments.text: President Trump reacted to the decision with sharp criticism of the court, specifically targeting his own appointees, Justices Gorsuch and Barrett. Despite the ruling, the President signaled he would pursue tariffs through alternative legal avenues, such as Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974. The legal defeat comes amid reports of slowing economic growth, with the GDP rising at a 1.4% annualized rate in the fourth quarter of 2025.

citations.quote: He reserved particular wrath for two of his appointees, Justices Neil Gorsuch and Amy Coney Barrett, calling them an ’embarrassment to their families.’
the Bureau of Economic Analysis announced the gross domestic product had grown at just a 1.4% annualized rate in the fourth quarter.

segments.heading: Potential Supreme Court Vacancies and Foreign Policy
segments.text: During a visit to Corpus Christi, Texas, President Trump discussed the possibility of nominating Senator Ted Cruz to the Supreme Court. The President joked that Cruz would receive unanimous bipartisan support because lawmakers would want him out of the Senate. Additionally, the President addressed tensions with Iran, noting that while military strikes remain an option, he would prefer a peaceful resolution to the ongoing nuclear capability concerns.

citations.quote: I'm thinking about putting him in the Supreme Court. … Getting these nominations through is very tough, and he's the only guy I know who'll get 100% of the Democrat vote, 100% of the Republican vote. They want to get him out of there.
Trump told reporters earlier Friday he had not yet decided if he would launch military strikes on Iran, but he voiced his unhappiness with recent negotiations.

story.heading: Investigation Continues into Disappearance of Nancy Guthrie in Arizona
story.summary: Authorities in Tucson, Arizona, are entering the third week of an intensive search for 84-year-old Nancy Guthrie, who was reported missing from her home on January 31 under circumstances investigators believe involve abduction.

segments.heading: Disappearance and Initial Evidence
segments.text: Nancy Guthrie was last seen on January 31 at her home in the Catalina Foothills. Evidence suggests she was removed from the residence against her will, leaving behind her mobile phone and essential medications. Investigators have identified a masked individual on doorbell camera footage tampering with security equipment while carrying an Ozark-brand backpack. Additionally, neighbors have provided Ring camera footage that may show vehicles on a route leading away from the crime scene.

citations.quote: Nancy Guthrie was last seen on January 31, before she was apparently kidnapped, disappearing from her secluded home in Arizona’s Catalina Foothills without her phone – or critical medications.
Nancy Guthrie neighbors' Ring camera captures vehicles on possible route from crime scene

segments.heading: Forensic Analysis and Investigative Leads
segments.text: The Pima County Sheriff's Department and the FBI are currently analyzing DNA evidence. While DNA was recovered from the Guthrie home, a separate sample found on gloves two miles away did not match the national CODIS database or the evidence found at the residence. Law enforcement officials are also reviewing thousands of hours of surveillance footage from the Tucson area. A suspicious individual was reportedly seen by a neighbor walking near the property two weeks prior to the disappearance.

citations.quote: DNA on gloves found 2 miles from the home did not return any matches in the national database known as CODIS, and doesn’t match DNA found the house, either
Nancy Guthrie's neighbor saw suspicious man walking nearby 2 weeks before suspected abduction

segments.heading: Scope of the Search
segments.text: The investigation has extended to the U.S.-Mexico border due to Tucson's proximity to the region. However, Mexican officials in the state of Sonora have stated there is currently no evidence suggesting Guthrie was transported across the border. Within the U.S., the Pima County Sheriff has explicitly stated that Guthrie's adult children are not considered suspects in the case. While ransom notes have been reported, authorities have not yet established a definitive motive.

citations.quote: The FBI informed us that they currently have no leads to suggest that this woman could be in Mexico
Nancy Guthrie’s adult children and their spouses are not suspects in her February 1 disappearance

story.heading: U.S. Deploys F-22 Stealth Fighters to Israel Amid Rising Tensions with Iran
story.summary: The United States has stationed F-22 Raptor stealth fighters in Israel for the first time, a move intended to enhance regional deterrence as the Trump administration weighs its options regarding Iran's nuclear and missile programs.

segments.heading: Strategic Deployment of F-22 Raptors
segments.text: Eleven U.S. Air Force F-22 Raptors have arrived at Israel's Ovda Air Base. This marks the first operational deployment of these advanced combat aircraft to Israel. Military officials describe the F-22 as a critical asset for establishing air superiority and suppressing enemy air defenses, which would be essential in any potential strike scenario against hardened targets in Iran.

citations.quote: Eleven U.S. Air Force F-22 Raptor stealth fighters have arrived at Israel’s Ovda Air Base in the country’s south, Fox News reported, marking the first-ever operational deployment of American combat aircraft to Israel.
The F-22 is indeed the most air-to-air capable fighter in the world; nothing comes close to it in the air-to-air role.

segments.heading: U.S. Policy and Regional Objectives
segments.text: The Trump administration has indicated that while diplomacy is the preferred path, military force remains an option. The deployment is viewed by some military analysts as a way to expand presidential options and provide a 'clear deterrent signal.' Basing the aircraft in Israel is noted for offering fewer political constraints compared to some Gulf state bases, providing U.S. planners with greater freedom of action.

citations.quote: Trump signaled Friday that diplomacy remains his preference but did not rule out force.
Unlike some Gulf bases, where operational restrictions can apply, Israel offers fewer political constraints.

segments.heading: Iran's Retaliatory Capabilities
segments.text: Iran maintains a variety of options to respond to a potential strike, including a large inventory of ballistic missiles and drones capable of reaching U.S. bases and Israel. Despite being weakened by previous strikes and domestic issues, Tehran has demonstrated its ability to bypass sophisticated air defenses, as seen in past engagements. U.S. officials acknowledge that Iran's asymmetric capabilities make a decisive strike more complex.

citations.quote: Iran is believed to have thousands of missiles and drones within range of US troops based in a number of countries in the Middle East.
Tehran’s military capabilities, even if far outnumbered and much older than modern US systems, make a decisive US strike on the country far more difficult.

segments.heading: Proxy Networks and Regional Stability
segments.text: Iran's regional network of proxies, including groups in Iraq and Lebanon's Hezbollah, have pledged support to the Islamic Republic in the event of an attack. However, these groups face significant constraints following recent conflicts with Israel. Analysts suggest that Iran's response would likely depend on whether it perceives a conflict as a limited strike or an existential threat to the regime.

citations.quote: Iraqi groups like Kataeb Hezbollah and Harakat al-Nujaba — militias that have targeted US forces in the past — as well as Lebanon’s Hezbollah, have said they will come to Iran’s aid if it is attacked.
The regime has a lot of capabilities to use if they see this as an existential war.

story.heading: Trump Considers Military Strikes on Iran Amid Tariff Escalation and Legal Challenges
story.summary: President Donald Trump has expressed dissatisfaction with nuclear negotiations with Iran, weighing potential military strikes while simultaneously increasing global import tariffs to 15% following a Supreme Court ruling against his previous trade policy.

segments.heading: Iran Negotiations and Potential Military Action
segments.text: President Trump stated on Friday that he is dissatisfied with the progress of nuclear negotiations with Iran, noting that the country has not yet agreed to U.S. demands. While the president has not made a final decision regarding military strikes, he acknowledged the risk of a long-term conflict in the Middle East. Concurrently, the State Department authorized non-essential employees at the U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem to depart due to regional tensions, and Iran's foreign minister indicated a nuclear counterproposal is being prepared.

citations.quote: President Donald Trump told reporters on Friday that he is 'not happy' with the way Iran is negotiating.
Iran’s foreign minister has said he expects to have a draft counterproposal ready within days after nuclear talks with the US this week

segments.heading: Tariff Increases and Supreme Court Ruling
segments.text: Following a 6-3 Supreme Court ruling that limited the president's authority to impose tariffs under the 1977 International Emergency Economic Powers Act, Trump announced an increase in global import duties to 15%. The administration is utilizing an alternative legal avenue to implement these levies, which are scheduled to take effect on February 24. Business associations have responded by requesting refunds for approximately $133 billion in previously collected tariffs affected by the court's decision.

citations.quote: Trump raised the global duty on imports into the US to 15% on Saturday, doubling down on his promise to maintain his aggressive tariff policy a day after the supreme court ruled much of it illegal.

segments.heading: International and Domestic Reactions
segments.text: European leaders, including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and French President Emmanuel Macron, have expressed concern over the economic uncertainty and emphasized the need for reciprocity. Domestically, the administration faces criticism over the use of presidential imagery on federal buildings, while also managing local issues such as a federal emergency declaration for a sewage spill in the Potomac River.

citations.quote: German chancellor, Friedrich Merz, said he would travel to Washington with a coordinated European position, and warned of the ‘poison’ of more uncertainty.

story.heading: China Claims Gold and Bronze in Women's Aerials at 2026 Winter Olympics
story.summary: China's Xu Mengtao and Shao Qi secured podium finishes in the women's aerials competition in Livigno, while American favorites Kaila Kuhn and Winter Vinecki finished fifth and sixth after unsuccessful landings in the final round.

segments.heading: Competition Results and Performance
segments.text: The women's aerials final at the Livigno Aerials & Moguls Park saw China's Xu Mengtao take the gold medal with a score of 112.90. Australia's Danielle Scott earned silver with 102.17, and China's Shao Qi took the bronze with 101.90. The final round utilized a format where previous scores were discarded, leaving the six finalists to compete for medals based on a single jump.

citations.quote: China’s Xu Mengtao won gold with a 112.90 (up from 107.75), while Australia’s Danielle Scott (102.17) took silver, and China’s Shao Qi (101.90) the bronze.

segments.heading: U.S. Team Performance and Strategy
segments.text: Three Americans reached the 12-skier final field, with Kaila Kuhn and Winter Vinecki advancing to the final six. Both Kuhn and Vinecki attempted high-difficulty 'back-full-full-full' triple jumps in the medal round but failed to stick their landings. Kuhn finished in fifth place with a score of 99.16, while Vinecki finished sixth with 90.58. Tasia Tanner, who balances training with a full-time career as an IT manager, finished 11th.

citations.quote: Vinecki’s final score of 90.58 (down from 107.75) meant a sixth-place finish, with Kuhn’s 99.16 (down from 109.90) one spot ahead of her in fifth.
Tanner said she works full-time as an IT manager for Hilton Hotels, working at the Waldorf Astoria in Park City, Utah.

segments.heading: Olympic Context and Future Games
segments.text: The 2026 Winter Games are concluding with ceremonies approaching. Future Olympic hosts have already been designated, including Utah in the United States, the French Alps, Brisbane, and Los Angeles. The women's aerials event in Livigno faced scheduling challenges earlier in the week due to heavy snowfall, which forced qualifying and finals to be held on the same day.

citations.quote: Future Olympic hosts: BRISBANE, FRENCH ALPS, LOS ANGELES, UTAH
This women's aerials competition was postponed by heavy snow this week in Livigno, with qualifying moved to the same day as the finals.

story.heading: 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics Conclude with Historic Performances
story.summary: The 2026 Winter Games in Italy have reached their final stage, marked by record-breaking achievements in cross-country skiing and speed skating, alongside the conclusion of major team tournaments in hockey and curling.

segments.heading: Record-Breaking Individual Achievements
segments.text: Norway's Johannes H sflot Kl&bo made history by winning six gold medals in a single Winter Olympics, surpassing the previous record held by American Eric Heiden. Meanwhile, 21-year-old American speed skater Jordan Stolz concluded his campaign with two gold medals and one silver, setting Olympic records in the 500m and 1,000m events.

citations.quote: The 29-year-old Norwegian’s six gold medals in these Games are the most ever won in a single Olympics.
Stolz will attempt to become the first U.S. speed skater since Eric Heiden in 1980 to win four gold medals in the same Olympics

segments.heading: Men's Ice Hockey and Curling Results
segments.text: In men's ice hockey, the United States and Canada advanced to the gold medal game after the U.S. defeated Sweden in the quarterfinals and Canada secured the top seed through a goal differential tiebreaker. Finland secured the bronze medal with a 6-1 victory over Slovakia. In curling, Brad Jacobs led Canada to its first men's gold since 2014 by defeating Great Britain 9-6.

citations.quote: Canada received the top overall seed thanks to winning a tiebreaker against the United States for a better goal differential.
Finland put the disappointment of Friday’s narrow loss to Canada in the rearview mirror and blitzed Slovakia 6-1 to claim the bronze medal in men’s hockey.

segments.heading: Final Day Events and Gender Equality Milestones
segments.text: The final day of competition features the women's 50-kilometer mass start in cross-country skiing, the first time women have competed at this distance in the Olympics to promote gender balance. Other concluding events include the women's freestyle skiing halfpipe final, where Eileen Gu seeks to defend her title, and the women's curling gold medal match between Sweden and Switzerland.

citations.quote: This is the first time women will compete in the Olympics at 50km — a staple distance for the men — in support of making the 2026 Games what the International Olympic Committee has called ‘the most gender-balanced Olympic Winter Games in history.’

story.heading: Finland Secures Bronze as Men's Hockey Tournament Concludes at 2026 Winter Olympics
story.summary: The 2026 Winter Olympics men's ice hockey tournament saw Finland claim the bronze medal with a 6-1 victory over Slovakia, following a dramatic quarterfinal round where the United States, Canada, and Finland all advanced through overtime wins.

segments.heading: Quarterfinal Results and Overtime Drama
segments.text: The men's quarterfinals on February 18 featured three games decided in overtime. The United States advanced after Quinn Hughes scored the winning goal against Sweden in a 3-2 victory. Canada also moved forward with a 4-3 overtime win against Czechia, despite losing captain Sidney Crosby to an injury during the second period. Finland secured its place in the semifinals by overcoming a 2-0 deficit to defeat Switzerland 3-2 in extra time, while Slovakia advanced by defeating Germany 6-2.

citations.quote: Four games. Three went to overtime, including the USA beating Sweden.
Canada captain Sidney Crosby hobbled off the ice and down the tunnel after a hard hit by Radko Gudas... He eventually went to the dressing room and didn't return.

segments.heading: Finland Claims Bronze Medal
segments.text: After losing to Canada in the semifinals, Finland rebounded to defeat Slovakia 6-1 in the bronze-medal game. Sebastian Aho and Erik Haula provided early goals, and despite a second-period response from Slovak captain Tomš Tatar, Finland scored four unanswered goals in the third period to secure the win. This victory marks Finland's fifth medal in the last six Olympics featuring NHL players, a record that exceeds other major hockey nations like Canada and the United States during that span.

citations.quote: A 6-1 victory over upstart Slovakia that was closer than the final score suggests Saturday put the Finns where they always seem to be: on the Olympic podium.
This was Finland’s fifth medal in six Olympics with NHL participation — more than Canada, more than Sweden, more than the United States, more than anybody.

segments.heading: Scheduling Conflicts for U.S. Viewers
segments.text: Olympic broadcasting in the United States faced a notable scheduling overlap on Thursday, February 19. The women's ice hockey gold-medal game between the U.S. and Canada was scheduled to take place simultaneously with the women's figure skating finals. In figure skating, Alysa Liu entered the final in third place, representing the primary American hope for a medal in that event.

citations.quote: on Thursday, Feb. 19 the finals for women's ice hockey and women's figure skating take place at the same time during the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics.

story.summary: A summary of upcoming sports broadcasts including the 2026 Winter Olympics, NCAA basketball schedules for February 18, and a professional basketball matchup between Houston and New York on February 21.

segments.heading: 2026 Winter Olympics Coverage
segments.text: The 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics continue on Wednesday, February 18. NBC will broadcast replays of Team USA performances and key events from the day. Live coverage of the competitions is available through the Peacock streaming service.

citations.quote: The broadcast coverage of the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics continues on Wednesday, Feb. 18 with NBC airing a number of replays featuring Team USA's athletes and the day's biggest moments.

Both teams face roster challenges due to injuries: the Knicks will be without Miles McBride (ankle), while the Rockets have lost Fred VanVleet (ACL) and Steven Adams (ankle) for the remainder of the season.

citations.quote: The Houston Rockets (34-20) will attempt to build on a five-game road winning streak when they square off against the New York Knicks (35-21) on Feb. 21, 2026 at Madison Square Garden.

story.heading: Duke Defeats Michigan as NCAA Tournament Field Takes Shape
story.summary: No. 3 Duke secured a significant victory over No. 1 Michigan led by freshman Cameron Boozer, while the broader college basketball landscape shifts toward the Big Ten ahead of the upcoming NCAA Tournament.

segments.heading: Duke Secures Top-Ranked Victory
segments.text: In a highly anticipated matchup between two of the nation's top programs, No. 3 Duke defeated No. 1 Michigan with a 68-63 victory at Capital One Arena. Despite foul trouble that sidelined him for a portion of the second half, Duke freshman Cameron Boozer led his team with 18 points, 10 rebounds, and seven assists. The Blue Devils' defense held Michigan to 28.6 percent shooting in the second half, preventing the Wolverines from capitalizing when Boozer was on the bench.

citations.quote: Boozer led Duke in points (18), rebounds (10) and assists (seven) against the top-ranked team in the sport
overall defensive effort that held a top-10 offense to 28.6 percent shooting in the second half

segments.heading: NCAA Tournament Outlook and Conference Trends
segments.text: As the 2025-26 season approaches its conclusion, the Big Ten has emerged as a dominant force in the national picture, contrasting with the SEC's performance in the previous year. Current projections place Georgia, UCLA, Ohio State, and TCU among the last four teams to qualify for the tournament field. Conversely, teams such as New Mexico, California, Missouri, and Santa Clara are currently positioned just outside the qualifying bracket as conference tournaments begin.

citations.quote: That conference has taken a step back this year, with the Big Ten emerging as the conference to watch.
Georgia, UCLA, Ohio State, TCU are the last four teams in the tourney, through Tuesday, Feb. 17.

segments.heading: Impact of Freshman Talent
segments.text: The current college basketball season has been defined by a standout class of freshmen. Cameron Boozer has established himself as a front-runner for the Wooden Award, recording 15 games with at least 15 points and 10 rebounds. Other notable freshmen expected to impact the national tournament include Darryn Peterson and AJ Dybantsa, who remain high-level prospects for professional scouts.

citations.quote: Saturday was Boozer’s 15th game this season with at least 15 points and 10 rebounds, the most of any high-major player in America.
As March Madness approaches, a standout freshman class is already shaping the tournament picture.

story.heading: Supreme Court Rules Against Presidential Tariffs as Trump Hosts Governors
story.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has ruled that President Donald Trump's signature tariffs are unlawful, citing constitutional limits on executive power, while the President simultaneously hosted the National Governors Association amid partisan tensions.

segments.heading: Supreme Court Ruling on Executive Tariff Authority
segments.text: In a significant judicial decision, Chief Justice John Roberts announced that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act does not grant the President the authority to impose tariffs. The ruling, which spanned a 21-page opinion, emphasized that the power to tax resides with Congress rather than the executive branch. Roberts referenced historical precedent from 1824 to support the court's stance on the separation of powers, while noting that the court had previously rejected initiatives from the Biden administration as well.

citations.quote: Fulfilling that role, we hold that (the International Emergency Economic Powers Act) does not authorize the President to impose tariffs.
Two centuries ago, in 1824, Marshall had deemed the authority to impose tariffs ‘a branch of the taxing power,’ which is firmly the domain of Congress.

segments.heading: National Governors Association Dinner and Partisan Boycott
segments.text: President Trump addressed state leaders during the National Governors Association (NGA) dinner on Saturday, praising the work of the governors in attendance. However, the event was marked by a boycott from eighteen Democratic governors. This tension followed a dispute regarding invitations to a White House working breakfast; the administration initially invited only Republican governors before reversing course and inviting all state leaders, a move the administration described as a scheduling misunderstanding.

citations.quote: Eighteen Democratic governors said they were boycotting Saturday’s dinner, and some also said they wouldn’t go to the Friday morning meeting.
President Trump said this was always his intention, and we have addressed the misunderstanding in scheduling.

articles.title: Horoscopes Today, February 18, 2026
